project.name: PropManager Payment Workflow v2.0 - Critical Remediation
project.description: Critical security, idempotency, and architecture fixes identified during comprehensive audit. This is the BLOCKING release before production deployment. Includes database-level idempotency, credential migration to database, Strategy pattern refactoring, full webhook security suite, and comprehensive test coverage.
project.audit_source: Tracer bullet analysis found 70+ files touching payments

auditFindings.description: Critical gaps from tracer bullet analysis of 70+ payment files

critical.finding: No unique database constraint on mpesa_transaction_id
critical.risk: Race conditions can create duplicate payments despite pessimistic locking
critical.evidence: Grep found no unique() in migrations for M-Pesa columns

critical.finding: No unique database constraint on intasend_reference
critical.risk: Same race condition issue as M-Pesa
critical.evidence: Grep found no unique() in migrations for IntaSend columns

critical.finding: Security PRD (SEC-001 to SEC-007) NOT STARTED
critical.risk: Per-tenant credentials in .env violates multi-tenant SaaS security
critical.evidence: All SEC items have passes:false, attempt_count:0

critical.risk: Violates SRP, CLAUDE.md says max 300 lines

critical.finding: M-Pesa IP validation only warns, doesn't reject
critical.risk: Spoofed webhooks from non-Safaricom IPs still processed

researchSources.title: Idempotency in Payment Processing
researchSources.key_insight: Use BOTH unique constraints AND early-insert pattern

researchSources.title: Airbnb Idempotency Pattern
researchSources.key_insight: Insert idempotency record BEFORE processing, not after

researchSources.title: Laravel Payment Gateway with Strategy Pattern
researchSources.key_insight: Strategy + Adapter pattern for SOLID compliance

researchSources.title: M-Pesa Production Checklist
researchSources.key_insight: Timestamp + IP + signature validation for webhooks

userDecisions.description: Decisions gathered from user through comprehensive AskUserQuestion sessions

qualityStandards.description: Enhanced standards based on audit findings and research
principles: Database-level unique constraints for ALL payment idempotency keys
Early-insert idempotency pattern: insert record BEFORE processing
Per-tenant credentials stored encrypted in database, NEVER in .env
Controllers < 300 lines - extract to services with Strategy pattern
80% minimum test coverage on payment-related code
Reject unknown webhook IPs with 403 (fail-closed security)
Amount validation with 1 KES tolerance on ALL webhooks
Timestamp validation: reject callbacks >15 minutes old
Payment intent pattern: create pending record BEFORE external API call
Exponential backoff retry for network failures (1s, 2s, 4s)
Dead letter queue for failed webhooks with email alerts
Document all architectural decisions in docs/adr/
Rollback plan required for every migration

feedbackLoops.description: Run ALL feedback loops before marking any item complete

before_commit: Unique constraints exist on all payment reference columns

workflow.description: Enhanced TDD workflow with ADR requirement

steps.name: Write Failing Test
steps.description: Before writing any implementation code, create test(s) that verify the acceptance criteria.
steps.example: test('webhook with timestamp >15 min returns 403', function() { ... })
checklist: Test file exists in tests/Feature/ or tests/Unit/
Test fails for the right reason

steps.name: Minimal Implementation
steps.description: Write the minimum code to make the test pass.

steps.name: Refactor
steps.description: Clean up while keeping tests green. Document architectural decisions.

steps.name: Document Rollback
steps.description: For migrations, document explicit rollback steps.

steps.name: Verify
steps.description: Run full suite and manual verification.

idempotency.description: Database-level idempotency with early-insert pattern - BLOCKING

security.description: Credential migration and webhook security - BLOCKING

architecture.description: Strategy pattern refactoring and service extraction

webhook_robustness.description: IP rejection, amount validation, dead letter queue

test_coverage.description: Fill coverage gaps for ALL payment files

items.title: Add Unique Constraint on mpesa_transaction_id with Early-Insert Pattern
items.description: Add database-level unique constraint AND implement early-insert pattern. Currently only pessimistic locking is used which can fail under high concurrency. Research shows inserting idempotency record BEFORE processing prevents ALL race conditions.
steps: Create migration 2026_02_02_000001_add_unique_constraint_mpesa_transaction_id.php
Add audit query to check for existing duplicates: SELECT mpesa_transaction_id, COUNT(*) FROM payments WHERE mpesa_transaction_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY mpesa_transaction_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
If duplicates exist, migration MUST FAIL with clear error listing duplicate IDs
Add unique index: $table->unique('mpesa_transaction_id') at line after index definition
Document rollback: $table->dropUnique('payments_mpesa_transaction_id_unique')
Update MpesaWebhookController::processPayment() (line ~170) to INSERT payment record with status='pending' BEFORE processing
Catch UniqueConstraintViolationException, return 200 OK (idempotent behavior)
Create ADR: docs/adr/006-payment-idempotency-pattern.md documenting early-insert approach
definition_of_ready: Audit query run on production database to identify existing duplicates
If duplicates exist, manual resolution plan documented
Team understands early-insert pattern from research
acceptance_criteria: SHOW INDEX FROM payments shows unique constraint on mpesa_transaction_id
Duplicate M-Pesa webhooks return 200 OK without creating duplicate payment
Migration fails with clear message listing duplicate IDs if any exist
Payment record created with status='pending' BEFORE calling M-Pesa APIs
50+ concurrent webhooks with same transaction_id creates exactly 1 payment
definition_of_done: Migration runs successfully on clean database
SHOW INDEX FROM payments shows unique on mpesa_transaction_id
Feature test: 50 concurrent webhooks creates exactly 1 payment
Unit test: early-insert creates pending record before processing
items.verification: php artisan migrate; SHOW INDEX FROM payments; run ConcurrentWebhookTest 5 times to ensure no flakiness.

rollback_plan.sql: ALTER TABLE payments DROP INDEX payments_mpesa_transaction_id_unique
rollback_plan.verification: SHOW INDEX FROM payments should not show unique on mpesa_transaction_id
rollback_plan.notes: Rollback is safe - unique constraint removal doesn't affect data

items.implementation_notes: Migration adds unique constraint with duplicate check. MpesaWebhookController updated with explicit QueryException handling (error 1062). Also fixed tillConfirmation() idempotency gap. ADR-006 documented at docs/adr/006-payment-idempotency-pattern.md. All 7 tests passing (MpesaIdempotencyTest).

items.title: Add Unique Constraint on intasend_reference with Early-Insert Pattern
items.description: Same pattern as PAY-V2-001 for IntaSend. Add unique constraint and early-insert for bulletproof idempotency.
steps: Create migration 2026_02_02_000002_add_unique_constraint_intasend_reference.php
Audit for existing duplicates
Add unique index: $table->unique('intasend_reference')
Update IntaSendWebhookController::processCompletePayment() (line ~195) for early-insert
Handle UniqueConstraintViolationException gracefully
definition_of_ready: Audit query run for IntaSend duplicates
acceptance_criteria: Duplicate webhooks return 200 OK without duplicate payment
definition_of_done: SHOW INDEX shows unique on intasend_reference
Feature test: concurrent webhooks handled correctly
items.verification: php artisan migrate; run concurrent webhook test.

rollback_plan.sql: ALTER TABLE payments DROP INDEX payments_intasend_reference_unique
rollback_plan.verification: SHOW INDEX should not show unique on intasend_reference

items.implementation_notes: Migration adds unique constraint with duplicate check (2026_02_04_000001). IntaSendWebhookController updated with QueryException(1062) handler. Security fix: intasend_webhook_challenge now encrypted in PaymentConfiguration. ADR-006 updated with IntaSend pattern. All 6 tests passing (IntaSendIdempotencyTest). Also fixed pre-existing MpesaIdempotencyTest route bug for till confirmation.

items.title: Create Idempotency Key Table for Cross-Request Synchronization
items.description: Research shows idempotency table prevents duplicate processing BEFORE database insert. Create dedicated table for idempotency keys with TTL.
steps: Create migration 2026_02_02_000003_create_idempotency_keys_table.php
Columns: id, key (unique), request_hash, status (pending/processing/completed/failed), response_data (json), created_at, expires_at
Create app/Models/IdempotencyKey.php with scopes: active(), expired()
Create app/Services/IdempotencyService.php with acquire(key), release(key, response), isProcessing(key)
Implement in all webhook controllers as FIRST step
Add scheduled job to cleanup expired keys (>24 hours)
acceptance_criteria: Idempotency key checked BEFORE any processing
Concurrent requests with same key: first processes, others wait/return cached response
items.verification: Feature test: send same webhook twice simultaneously, verify only one processes.

rollback_plan.code_changes: Remove IdempotencyService calls from webhook controllers

items.implementation_notes: Migration creates idempotency_keys table with unique key, status enum, response_data JSON, and TTL. IdempotencyKey model with scopes (active, expired, pending, completed). IdempotencyService with acquire/release/fail/cleanupExpired methods. CleanupExpiredIdempotencyKeys command scheduled daily at 03:00. ADR-006 updated with two-layer idempotency architecture and controller integration details. CONTROLLER INTEGRATION COMPLETE: MpesaWebhookController (processPayment, tillConfirmation), IntaSendWebhookController (processCompletePayment), PaymentController (processSuccessfulCharge), BankWebhookController (processWebhook + FIXED missing QueryException handler). 54 idempotency tests passing (17 unit, 18 feature integration, 19 provider tests). 5 E2E browser tests added for frontend verification.

items.title: Migrate Paystack Credentials to Database (SEC-001)
items.description: Move PAYSTACK_PUBLIC_KEY and PAYSTACK_SECRET_KEY from .env to payment_configurations table. Each landlord configures their own Paystack account via Settings UI.
steps: Create migration 2026_02_02_000004_add_paystack_keys_to_payment_configurations.php
Add columns: paystack_public_key (string), paystack_secret_key (string, encrypted cast)
Add hasPaystackFullConfig() helper method to PaymentConfiguration model (line ~150)
Update PaystackService constructor to accept PaymentConfiguration (line ~45)
Add withConfig(PaymentConfiguration $config) method for per-landlord config
Implement fallback to .env if database config not set (backward compatibility)
Add deprecation warning when using .env: Log::warning('Using deprecated .env Paystack config...')
Update PaymentMethodsTab.vue to show Paystack public key input (full) and secret key input (last 4 chars only)
Write tests for both DB config and .env fallback scenarios
acceptance_criteria: Paystack keys stored encrypted in database (encrypted cast verified)
Landlords can configure keys via Settings > Payment Methods
Secret key shows as ****xxxx (last 4 chars) in UI
NO .env fallback (security: multi-tenant SaaS requires per-landlord isolation)
Webhook handler verifies signature with correct landlord's secret key
definition_of_done: Settings UI shows Paystack configuration with masked secret
Tests pass for both DB and .env scenarios
Manual test: configure Paystack in UI, make payment
items.verification: Configure Paystack keys in Settings UI; initiate payment; verify keys used from DB not .env.

rollback_plan.sql: ALTER TABLE payment_configurations DROP COLUMN paystack_public_key, DROP COLUMN paystack_secret_key
rollback_plan.code_changes: Revert PaystackService to use only .env config

items.completion_notes: 2026-02-04: Implemented WITHOUT .env fallback (security fix). Webhook handler now extracts landlord_id from metadata and verifies signature with correct per-landlord secret. Settings controller sanitizes secrets and adds last4 display. 12/12 feature tests pass.

items.title: Migrate M-Pesa Consumer Credentials to Database (SEC-002)
items.description: Move MPESA_CONSUMER_KEY and MPESA_CONSUMER_SECRET from .env to payment_configurations. These are Safaricom Daraja app credentials per landlord.
steps: Create migration 2026_02_02_000005_add_mpesa_consumer_to_payment_configurations.php
Add columns: mpesa_consumer_key, mpesa_consumer_secret (encrypted)
Update MpesaService::getAccessToken() (line ~85) to use landlord credentials
Update cache key to include landlord_id to prevent cross-tenant token sharing
Update PaymentMethodsTab.vue with M-Pesa API credentials section
Maintain backward compatibility with .env fallback
acceptance_criteria: Consumer credentials stored encrypted per landlord
OAuth token generation uses landlord credentials
definition_of_done: STK Push works with per-landlord credentials
items.verification: Configure M-Pesa keys for 2 landlords; verify token cache is separate; initiate STK Push for each.

rollback_plan.sql: ALTER TABLE payment_configurations DROP COLUMN mpesa_consumer_key, DROP COLUMN mpesa_consumer_secret

items.implementation_notes: Fixed TenantPaymentController::checkMpesaStatus() to load PaymentConfiguration and pass to MpesaService::querySTKStatus(). Added withConfig() call in querySTKStatus() and initiateSTKPush() before getAccessToken(). Added missing mpesa_shortcode/passkey/environment fields to UpdatePaymentMethodsRequest validation. Created MpesaCredentialMigrationTest (12 tests) and MpesaCredentialSettingsTest (E2E).

items.title: Migrate M-Pesa B2C Credentials to Database (SEC-003)
items.description: Move MPESA_B2C_* variables (initiator, password, security_credential) from .env to payment_configurations. B2C is used for refunds.
steps: Create migration 2026_02_02_000006_add_mpesa_b2c_to_payment_configurations.php
Add columns: mpesa_b2c_initiator, mpesa_b2c_password (encrypted), mpesa_b2c_security_credential (encrypted)
Update MpesaService B2C methods (line ~350) to use landlord config
Add B2C section to PaymentMethodsTab.vue
items.verification: Configure B2C credentials; initiate test refund (sandbox).

rollback_plan.sql: ALTER TABLE payment_configurations DROP COLUMN mpesa_b2c_initiator, DROP COLUMN mpesa_b2c_password, DROP COLUMN mpesa_b2c_security_credential

items.title: Create PaymentGatewayInterface and Strategy Pattern Foundation
items.description: Refactor payment gateway architecture to use Strategy + Adapter pattern following SOLID principles. Currently PaymentGatewayManager uses Factory pattern which is less flexible.
steps: Create app/Contracts/PaymentGatewayInterface.php with methods: getIdentifier(), isConfigured(), initializePayment(PaymentRequest), verifyPayment(string $reference), refundPayment(Payment), validateWebhook(Request), getPublicConfig()
Create app/ValueObjects/PaymentRequest.php with amount, currency, reference, metadata, callback_url
Create app/ValueObjects/PaymentResult.php with success, reference, provider_reference, raw_response, error_message
Update PaymentGatewayManager to use Strategy pattern (line ~25)
Create app/Services/Payment/AbstractPaymentGateway.php base class with shared logic
Create ADR: docs/adr/007-payment-gateway-strategy-pattern.md
definition_of_ready: Research on Strategy pattern for payments reviewed
acceptance_criteria: New gateways can be added without modifying existing code (OCP)
items.verification: Add mock gateway implementing interface; verify it works without changing existing code.

Move logic from PaymentController::storeManual() (lines 97-202)
Include: validation, transaction creation, invoice update, overpayment handling, event dispatch
Keep controller method to 15-20 lines: validate, delegate, return response
Create unit tests for service
Ensure PaymentObserver hooks still fire
definition_of_ready: Test coverage for current functionality documented
acceptance_criteria: Service handles all manual payment logic
Unit tests cover happy path and edge cases
definition_of_done: Unit tests pass with 90% coverage on service
items.verification: Record manual payment; verify invoice updated, event fired, receipt generated.

Move validateBulkImport() logic (lines 650-750)
Move processBulkImport() logic (lines 750-900)
Move processCurrentImport() logic (lines 900-1000)
Move processHistoricalImport() logic (lines 1000-1100)

Fixed legacy template bug (all 3 controllers now use modern templated-receipt). Added streamPreviewPdf() to ReceiptService. 7 unit tests + 7 feature tests + 4 E2E tests all pass. No PHPMD violations.

items.title: Implement Per-Gateway Retry Configuration
items.description: Add configurable retry policies per payment gateway based on provider reliability.
steps: Add retry config to config/payments.php: paystack.retry_attempts (3), mpesa.retry_attempts (5), intasend.retry_attempts (3)
Add retry_backoff_base per gateway
Update PaystackService, MpesaService, IntaSendService to read from config
Create config/payments.php if not exists

items.title: Implement Full Webhook Security Suite (M-Pesa + Paystack IP Validation)
items.description: Enterprise-grade webhook security for ALL payment providers. M-Pesa: timestamp + IP + signature validation. Paystack: IP whitelisting + HMAC signature (already in PAY-V2-010). Currently M-Pesa only warns on unknown IPs. Paystack has NO IP validation. Paystack official docs specify webhook IPs: 52.31.139.75, 52.49.173.169, 52.214.14.220.
steps: Update MpesaWebhookController to return 403 Forbidden for unknown IPs (line ~35)
Add timestamp validation: reject callbacks with timestamp >15 minutes old
Extract timestamp from callback body (TransactionDate field)
Add request signing verification if available from Safaricom
Create app/Http/Middleware/ValidateMpesaWebhook.php with all checks
Apply middleware to M-Pesa webhook routes
Add Paystack IP whitelist: 52.31.139.75, 52.49.173.169, 52.214.14.220 (from Paystack official docs)
Create app/Http/Middleware/ValidatePaystackWebhook.php with IP check
Apply Paystack middleware to POST /webhooks/paystack route
Log all rejected webhook requests with IP and reason for both providers
Tests for Paystack IP validation (valid IP passes, unknown IP gets 403)
acceptance_criteria: Unknown IPs receive 403 Forbidden (not just warning) for M-Pesa
Valid Safaricom IPs with recent timestamp processed
Paystack webhooks from non-whitelisted IPs receive 403 Forbidden
IP whitelist configurable via config/payments.php for both providers
All IP rejections logged with provider, IP, and reason
definition_of_done: Paystack IP middleware created and applied
403 returned for invalid requests from both providers
Config-driven IP whitelist for both M-Pesa and Paystack
items.verification: Send webhook from non-Safaricom IP; verify 403. Send webhook with old timestamp; verify 403. Send Paystack webhook from non-whitelisted IP; verify 403.

items.title: Create Dead Letter Queue Table and Model

Columns: id, landlord_id (FK), provider (mpesa/paystack/intasend), event_type, payload (json), error_reason, attempts (int), resolved_at, resolved_by, resolution_notes, created_at, updated_at
Create app/Models/WebhookDeadLetter.php
Add scopes: unresolved(), byProvider(string), recentFirst()
Add TenantScope trait for multi-tenant isolation
definition_of_done: Tinker test: can create and query dead letters
items.verification: php artisan migrate; Tinker: create dead letter, verify scopes work.

Implement capture(provider, payload, error): WebhookDeadLetter
Implement resolve(deadLetter, notes): void
Create app/Mail/FailedWebhookAlert.php mailable
Send alert to landlord email + admin email when captured
Add dead letter capture to all webhook controller catch blocks
acceptance_criteria: Failed webhooks captured with full payload
Email alert sent to landlord + admin
Dead letters can be marked resolved
Tests cover capture and alert scenarios
items.verification: Trigger webhook failure; verify dead letter created and email sent.

items.title: Add Amount Validation with 1 KES Tolerance to All Webhooks
items.description: Validate payment amount matches expected invoice amount within 1 KES tolerance. Currently only IntaSend has this check.
steps: Add amount validation to MpesaWebhookController::processPayment() (line ~200)
Add amount validation to PaymentCallbackProcessor (for Paystack)
Compare: abs($webhookAmount - $expectedAmount) <= 1.00
If mismatch > tolerance: log error, send to dead letter queue, return 200 OK
Create tests for exact, within-tolerance, and out-of-tolerance scenarios
acceptance_criteria: Amount validated on all webhook handlers
Mismatches sent to dead letter queue
definition_of_done: Amount validation in M-Pesa and Paystack handlers
items.verification: Send webhook with amount 0.50 KES under; verify accepted. Send webhook 1.50 KES under; verify rejected to dead letter.

items.title: Implement Webhook Retry Tracking
items.description: Track how many times providers retry the same webhook. Helps identify flaky webhooks and provider issues.

Columns: id, landlord_id, provider, event_id, payload_hash, retry_count, first_received_at, last_received_at, status (pending/processed/failed), processing_time_ms
Create app/Models/WebhookLog.php
Update webhook controllers to log and increment retry_count on duplicate event_id
definition_of_ready: Event ID field identified for each provider
acceptance_criteria: All webhooks logged with retry count
Retry count incremented on duplicate events
Can query high-retry webhooks for investigation
definition_of_done: Logging implemented in all webhook controllers
items.verification: Send same webhook 3 times; verify retry_count = 3 in log.

items.title: Implement Rate Limiting Per Invoice
items.description: Prevent payment abuse: 1 payment attempt per invoice per 60 seconds.
steps: Create RateLimiter for payment initiation in AppServiceProvider::boot()
Key: 'payment:' . $invoiceId
Limit: 1 per 60 seconds
Apply middleware to STK Push, Paystack init, IntaSend init endpoints
Return 429 Too Many Requests with retry-after header
acceptance_criteria: Rate limiting applied to all payment initiation endpoints
items.verification: Initiate payment for same invoice twice within 60s; verify second returns 429.

items.title: Create Health Check Endpoints for Payment Gateways
items.description: Add /health/payments endpoint showing status of each configured gateway. Useful for monitoring.
steps: Create app/Http/Controllers/HealthCheckController.php
Implement payments() method returning status of each gateway
For each gateway: check isConfigured(), optionally ping API (with cache)
Add route: GET /health/payments (public, no auth)
Return JSON: { paystack: 'ok', mpesa: 'degraded', intasend: 'not_configured' }

items.title: Fix mpesa_environment .env Security Violation (PAY-V2-005 Regression)
items.description: PAY-V2-005 migrated M-Pesa consumer credentials to database but missed mpesa_environment. The form validates it, but no DB column exists, $fillable doesn't include it, and MpesaService::withConfig() ignores it. ALL landlords share the global MPESA_ENVIRONMENT .env value. This violates multi-tenant SaaS isolation. IntaSend correctly has per-landlord intasend_environment — M-Pesa must match.
steps: Create migration: add mpesa_environment column to payment_configurations (nullable, default 'sandbox')
Add 'mpesa_environment' to PaymentConfiguration $fillable
Add MPESA_ENVIRONMENTS constant to PaymentConfiguration (mirrors INTASEND_ENVIRONMENTS)
Update MpesaService::withConfig() to set $this->environment and $this->baseUrl from PaymentConfiguration when mpesa_environment is not null
Write tests: verify per-landlord environment isolation (landlord A in sandbox, landlord B in production)
definition_of_ready: Confirmed: mpesa_environment column does not exist in any migration
Confirmed: MpesaService::withConfig() does not set environment
Confirmed: IntaSend pattern exists as reference
acceptance_criteria: mpesa_environment column exists in payment_configurations table
MpesaService::withConfig() sets $this->baseUrl to correct sandbox/production URL
Landlord A in sandbox and landlord B in production operate independently
definition_of_done: No per-tenant credentials or environment settings in .env
items.verification: Create 2 landlords: one sandbox, one production. Verify MpesaService uses correct base URL for each.

rollback_plan.sql: ALTER TABLE payment_configurations DROP COLUMN mpesa_environment

items.title: Extract InitialPaymentCallbackHandler from PaymentController
items.description: Extract handleInitialPaymentCallback() (84 lines) from PaymentController to a dedicated service. This method handles tenant onboarding payment verification via Paystack and bypasses PaymentCallbackProcessor. It has complex logic including duplicate detection, kobo conversion, TenantPaymentVerification management, and auto-approval with email notification.
steps: Create app/Services/Payment/InitialPaymentCallbackHandler.php
Move handleInitialPaymentCallback() logic (PaymentController lines 616-699)
Service takes (array $data, array $metadata) and returns result object
Include: duplicate detection, kobo->KES conversion, verification record update, auto-approval, email sending
Keep controller method to 5-10 lines: delegate to service, map result to redirect
Create unit tests covering: successful payment, duplicate reference, partial payment, full payment auto-approval, verification not found
Consider integrating with PaymentCallbackProcessor for consistency (currently bypassed)
acceptance_criteria: Service handles all initial payment callback logic
Tests cover happy path and all edge cases
items.verification: Process initial payment callback; verify verification record updated, auto-approval triggers, email sent.
